tool.name: trimest
tool.summary: Remove terminal poly-A tails from nucleotide sequence records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `trimest` removes bounded terminal poly-A tails from nucleotide sequence records. The Epithema v1 surface trims only trailing 3' runs of `A` when they meet a configured minimum length, keeping the behavior explicit and reproducible.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local nucleotide sequence input and an optional `--min-tail` threshold. Input records must be nucleotide-like; protein records are rejected.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits trimmed FASTA records in input order. CLI summaries report the source input, minimum tail threshold, exact trim rule, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema trimest`. Validation currently covers deterministic terminal poly-A trimming against committed FASTA fixtures in the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 implementation trims trailing `A` runs only. It does not attempt bidirectional EST cleanup, adapter detection, internal homopolymer trimming, or annotation-coordinate remapping, and it drops feature annotations after editing.

artifacts.label: trimest FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA input fixture used for deterministic terminal poly-A trimming validation.

examples.title: Trim terminal poly-A tails when they meet the minimum length
examples.description: Runs `trimest` against the committed FASTA fixture with `--min-tail 4`.

expected_outputs.label: Trimmed nucleotide records
expected_outputs.description: The output FASTA records contain residues `ACGT` and `TTGCAAA`, showing that only the first record met the four-residue trailing poly-A threshold.
